reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove that the group of invertible 2×2 matrices over Z_2 is isomorphic to S_3. In Lean, `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup (Fin 2) (ZMod 2)` is exactly GL(2, Z_2), i.e. the group of 2×2 matrices over Z_2 that are invertible (equivalent to having nonzero determinant, since Z_2 is a field). `Equiv.Perm (Fin 3)` is S_3, the symmetric group on 3 elements. The connective `≃*` is `MulEquiv`, which is group isomorphism, and wrapping it in `Nonempty` expresses "there exists such an isomorphism," which is the standard meaning of "is isomorphic to." So the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
